rationale.R1.1: Strengthening an academic abstract centrally requires sharpening the gap statement and contribution claim; the 'first work to apply iterative LLM self-feedback to climate narrative generation' framing is underdeveloped and needs reconstruction to be compelling.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the artifact contains a direct internal contradiction — paragraph 2 asserts 'no external retrieval at any stage,' yet paragraph 4 states the system 'queries during each critique step' retrieved IPCC AR6 excerpts and that 'this retrieval pipeline is the primary driver of the hallucination reduction.' This logical failure must be resolved for any strengthening to hold.
rationale.R1.3: The 12%→4% hallucination reduction and 50-municipality scope are quantitative claims central to the paper's persuasiveness; making the artifact stronger requires ensuring these are sufficiently grounded and not asserted without qualification.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim ('to our knowledge, first work to...') is the artifact's primary competitive stake; strengthening the paper requires assessing whether this claim survives contact with adjacent literature (iterative self-refinement, chain-of-thought critique loops) and tightening the delta framing.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is a standard load of 'make it stronger' delegations; the prose contains structural redundancy, hedging, and mid-paragraph contradiction that undercut authority and readability.
